name: PULMONARY VENO-OCCLUSIVE DISEASE
definition: fibrous narrowing / occlusion of pulmonary venules + small veins; the postcapillary

demographics.Age: < 20 years in 30-50% (range, 9 days to 70 years); M÷F = 1÷1

discriminating_features: diffuse / geographic / mosaic / perihilar / patchy / centrilobular ground-glass opacity

CXR: central and gravity-dependent parenchymal ground-glass attenuation
multifocal airspace consolidation ← parenchymal hemorrhage, pulmonary edema,
mediastinal lymphadenopathy ← vascular transformation of lymph node sinus with various
CT: diffuse / geographic / mosaic / perihilar / patchy / centrilobular ground-glass opacity
right heart enlargement + normal-sized left atrium
NUC: patchy distribution of 99mTc-MAA (of "upstream" pulmonary arterial hypertension)
ANGIO: enlarged right ventricle + central pulmonary arteries
delayed filling of normal pulmonary veins

clinical.Cx: potentially fatal pulmonary edema following administration of vasodilators for
clinical.Prognosis: death within 3 years ← NO effective therapy
clinical.Rx: lung / heart-lung transplant (only curative option); supportive diuretics, anticholinesterase
